name: generate_wiki_page
description: Generate (or refresh) a structured Markdown reference wiki page about a topic, synthesised from KB content. The page is persisted and returned immediately. Subsequent calls for the same topic return the cached version — pass force_refresh=true to regenerate. Use for: 'Create a wiki page about X', 'What does my KB say about Y?', 'Give me a reference page on Z'. Returns the full Markdown content plus metadata. Token cost: ~7K per generation (on-demand only).

topic.description: Topic to generate a wiki page about (e.g. 'Fed rate policy', 'requirements elicitation', 'AI regulation'). Can be a natural-language phrase.

force_refresh.description: If true, regenerate the page even if one already exists. Increments the version counter. Default: false (return cached if available).

user_id.description: User isolation key. Pass from session context.

page_id.description: UUID of the persisted wiki page.

title.description: Human-readable page title extracted from the generated Markdown H1.

content.description: Full Markdown content of the wiki page.

version.description: Page version (increments each time force_refresh generates new content).

source_chunk_count.description: Number of KB chunks used to generate this page.

cached.description: True if the page was returned from the cache without a new LLM call.

error.description: Non-null if generation failed. Inform the user and suggest they ingest relevant content first.
